title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: Schrödinger 套件是药物发现的工业标准，但它的数据格式 .mae（Maestro）是个黑箱： 私有格式：不是 PDB、不是 SDF，主流 R 包（Bio3D、ChemmineR）都读不了 Python 锁定：官方只提供 Python API，而且必须用 $SCHRODINGER/run 启动 环境冲突：Schrödinger 的 Python 环境和系统 Python / conda 互相打架 rschrodinger 用三种策略解决这些问题：

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
code: # 从 GitHub 安装 # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jixing475/rschrodinger") library(rschrodinger) library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE) library(tidyr) library(ggplot2)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 这是这个包的核心功能。零依赖，纯 R 实现，直接把 Maestro 文件变成 tidy tibble。 每一行是一个 CT（Connection Table） 块。对于 PrepWizard 输出的蛋白，通常只有 1 个 CT；对于 Glide docking 结果，每个 pose 是一个 CT。 这才是真正有用的场景 — 从 _pv.maegz 文件中提取所有 docking scores： 326 个 properties — 这就是 .mae 格式的威力。每个 CT 块带上百个属性：GlideScore、力场分解、每个残基的相互作用能量。 通常只需要几个核心 score。用 properties 参数精准提取： .mae 属性名有类型前缀（r_i_glide_gscore、s_m_title），默认会被清理掉。需要保留原始名称时： 一个目录下的所有 .mae/.maegz 文件打包读入： 做结构分析时用 include_atoms = TRUE：
code:
# 包内自带的示例文件 mae_file <- system.file("extdata", "example.mae",   package = "rschrodinger", mustWork = TRUE )  result <- read_mae_fast(mae_file) result # 真实的 Glide SP docking 结果（BRD4 × Indomethacin） # 这里替换为你自己跑完 Glide 输出的 pv.maegz 文件路径 maegz_file <- "/path/to/your/BRD4_Indomethacin_SP_pv.maegz" docking <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file) cat("CT blocks:", nrow(docking), "
") cat("Properties:", ncol(docking), "
") scores <- read_mae_fast(   maegz_file,   properties = c("title", "glide_gscore", "docking_score", "glide_emodel",                   "glide_lipo", "glide_hbond", "glide_evdw", "glide_ecoul") )  # 过滤掉受体 CT（第一个块，没有 glide_gscore） poses <- scores |>   filter(!is.na(glide_gscore)) |>   arrange(glide_gscore)  poses |>   select(title, glide_gscore, docking_score, glide_emodel) raw <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file, strip_prefix = FALSE) grep("glide", names(raw), value = TRUE) |> head(8) tmp_dir <- tempdir() file.copy(mae_file, file.path(tmp_dir, "protein_A.mae"), overwrite = TRUE) file.copy(mae_file, file.path(tmp_dir, "protein_B.mae"), overwrite = TRUE)  batch <- read_mae_fast(tmp_dir) batch |> select(`_source_file`, title) atoms <- read_mae_fast(mae_file, include_atoms = TRUE) atom_df <- atoms$atoms[[1]] cat("Atom count:", nrow(atom_df), "| Columns:", ncol(atom_df), "
") head(atom_df, 3)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 每一行是一个 CT（Connection Table） 块。对于 PrepWizard 输出的蛋白，通常只有 1 个 CT；对于 Glide docking 结果，每个 pose 是一个 CT。
code: # 包内自带的示例文件 mae_file <- system.file("extdata", "example.mae",   package = "rschrodinger", mustWork = TRUE )  result <- read_mae_fast(mae_file) result

what: 1.2 读取 Glide Docking 结果
title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 这才是真正有用的场景 — 从 _pv.maegz 文件中提取所有 docking scores： 326 个 properties — 这就是 .mae 格式的威力。每个 CT 块带上百个属性：GlideScore、力场分解、每个残基的相互作用能量。
code: # 真实的 Glide SP docking 结果（BRD4 × Indomethacin） # 这里替换为你自己跑完 Glide 输出的 pv.maegz 文件路径 maegz_file <- "/path/to/your/BRD4_Indomethacin_SP_pv.maegz" docking <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file) cat("CT blocks:", nrow(docking), "\n") cat("Properties:", ncol(docking), "\n")

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 通常只需要几个核心 score。用 properties 参数精准提取：
code: scores <- read_mae_fast(   maegz_file,   properties = c("title", "glide_gscore", "docking_score", "glide_emodel",                   "glide_lipo", "glide_hbond", "glide_evdw", "glide_ecoul") )  # 过滤掉受体 CT（第一个块，没有 glide_gscore） poses <- scores |>   filter(!is.na(glide_gscore)) |>   arrange(glide_gscore)  poses |>   select(title, glide_gscore, docking_score, glide_emodel)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: .mae 属性名有类型前缀（r_i_glide_gscore、s_m_title），默认会被清理掉。需要保留原始名称时：
code: raw <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file, strip_prefix = FALSE) grep("glide", names(raw), value = TRUE) |> head(8)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 一个目录下的所有 .mae/.maegz 文件打包读入：
code: tmp_dir <- tempdir() file.copy(mae_file, file.path(tmp_dir, "protein_A.mae"), overwrite = TRUE) file.copy(mae_file, file.path(tmp_dir, "protein_B.mae"), overwrite = TRUE)  batch <- read_mae_fast(tmp_dir) batch |> select(`_source_file`, title)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 做结构分析时用 include_atoms = TRUE：
code: atoms <- read_mae_fast(mae_file, include_atoms = TRUE) atom_df <- atoms$atoms[[1]] cat("Atom count:", nrow(atom_df), "| Columns:", ncol(atom_df), "\n") head(atom_df, 3)

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: Glide docking 的 _pv.maegz 文件里已经包含了残基级相互作用数据（258 列）。不需要 Schrödinger，直接提取： 上面的方法直接从已有数据提取。如果需要从任意 complex 结构做 de novo 相互作用分析（比如 MD 轨迹 snapshot），用 Schrödinger API 版本： 返回结构如下（mock 示例）：
code: # 读取完整数据 full <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file, strip_prefix = FALSE)  # 提取 best pose best_idx <- which.min(full$r_i_glide_gscore) best_pose <- full[best_idx, ] cat("Best pose GlideScore:", round(best_pose$r_i_glide_gscore, 2), "\n")  # 收集所有残基的 Eint（总相互作用能） eint_cols <- grep("^r_glide_res:.*_Eint$", names(best_pose), value = TRUE) eint_vals <- unlist(best_pose[, eint_cols]) residues <- gsub("r_glide_res:|_Eint", "", names(eint_vals)) names(eint_vals) <- residues  # 过滤有意义的相互作用 sig_interactions <- sort(eint_vals[!is.na(eint_vals) & abs(eint_vals) > 0.1])  interaction_df <- tibble(   residue = names(sig_interactions),   eint = as.numeric(sig_interactions) ) |>   arrange(eint)  interaction_df # 对 Top 10 残基做力场分解 top_res <- head(interaction_df$residue, 10)  decomp_data <- lapply(top_res, \(res) {   tibble(     residue = res,     vdw = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_vdw")]]),     coul = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_coul")]]),     hbond = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_hbond")]]),     lipo = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_lipo")]])   ) }) |> bind_rows()  decomp_long <- decomp_data |>   pivot_longer(-residue, names_to = "component", values_to = "energy") |>   mutate(     component = factor(component,       levels = c("vdw", "coul", "lipo", "hbond"),       labels = c("van der Waals", "Coulomb", "Lipophilic", "H-bond")     ),     residue = factor(residue, levels = rev(top_res))   )  ggplot(decomp_long, aes(x = component, y = residue, fill = energy)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_text(aes(label = round(energy, 1)),     size = 3, color = "white", fontface = "bold"   ) +   scale_fill_gradient2(     low = "#1e40af", mid = "#f8fafc", high = "#dc2626",     midpoint = 0, name = "kcal/mol"   ) +   labs(     title = "BRD4 × Indomethacin: Per-Residue Energy Decomposition",     subtitle = paste0("Best pose (GlideScore = ",       round(best_pose$r_i_glide_gscore, 2), ")"),     x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     panel.grid = element_blank(),     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)   ) # 需要 Schrödinger 安装 Sys.setenv(SCHRODINGER = "/opt/schrodinger2024-1")  interactions <- get_protein_ligand_interactions("my_complex.mae") interactions

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
code: # 对 Top 10 残基做力场分解 top_res <- head(interaction_df$residue, 10)  decomp_data <- lapply(top_res, \(res) {   tibble(     residue = res,     vdw = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_vdw")]]),     coul = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_coul")]]),     hbond = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_hbond")]]),     lipo = as.numeric(best_pose[[paste0("r_glide_res:", res, "_lipo")]])   ) }) |> bind_rows()  decomp_long <- decomp_data |>   pivot_longer(-residue, names_to = "component", values_to = "energy") |>   mutate(     component = factor(component,       levels = c("vdw", "coul", "lipo", "hbond"),       labels = c("van der Waals", "Coulomb", "Lipophilic", "H-bond")     ),     residue = factor(residue, levels = rev(top_res))   )  ggplot(decomp_long, aes(x = component, y = residue, fill = energy)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_text(aes(label = round(energy, 1)),     size = 3, color = "white", fontface = "bold"   ) +   scale_fill_gradient2(     low = "#1e40af", mid = "#f8fafc", high = "#dc2626",     midpoint = 0, name = "kcal/mol"   ) +   labs(     title = "BRD4 × Indomethacin: Per-Residue Energy Decomposition",     subtitle = paste0("Best pose (GlideScore = ",       round(best_pose$r_i_glide_gscore, 2), ")"),     x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     panel.grid = element_blank(),     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)   )

what: 2.2 用 get_protein_ligand_interactions() 做更细致的分析
title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 上面的方法直接从已有数据提取。如果需要从任意 complex 结构做 de novo 相互作用分析（比如 MD 轨迹 snapshot），用 Schrödinger API 版本： 返回结构如下（mock 示例）：
code: # 需要 Schrödinger 安装 Sys.setenv(SCHRODINGER = "/opt/schrodinger2024-1")  interactions <- get_protein_ligand_interactions("my_complex.mae") interactions

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 提交 Glide/Prime/Desmond 作业后查询状态： 返回结构：
code: # 单次查询 status <- poll_job_status("glide_SP_12345") status$status    # "running" / "completed" / "failed"  # 阻塞等待（适合脚本自动化） result <- poll_job_status(   "desmond_md_67890",   wait = TRUE,   timeout = 7200,   # 最多等 2 小时   interval = 60     # 每分钟查一次 )

what: 实战 Pipeline：Glide Docking 全景分析
title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: 把上面的功能串成一个完整的分析流程：
code: # Step 1: 读取 + 排序 poses <- read_mae_fast(maegz_file) |>   filter(!is.na(glide_gscore)) |>   mutate(label = paste0("Pose ", row_number())) |>   arrange(glide_gscore)  # Step 2: Score 排名可视化 ggplot(poses, aes(x = reorder(label, glide_gscore), y = glide_gscore)) +   geom_col(aes(fill = glide_gscore), show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "#1e40af", high = "#93c5fd") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "BRD4 × Indomethacin: Glide SP Docking Poses",     subtitle = paste0("10 poses | Best: ",       round(min(poses$glide_gscore), 2), " kcal/mol"),     x = NULL, y = "GlideScore (kcal/mol)"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()   ) # Step 3: 力场分解对比 energy_data <- poses |>   select(label, glide_lipo, glide_hbond, glide_evdw, glide_ecoul) |>   pivot_longer(-label, names_to = "component", values_to = "energy") |>   mutate(component = factor(     gsub("glide_", "", component),     levels = c("evdw", "ecoul", "lipo", "hbond"),     labels = c("vdW", "Coulomb", "Lipophilic", "H-bond")   ))  ggplot(energy_data, aes(x = label, y = energy, fill = component)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   coord_flip() +   labs(     title = "Energy Component Decomposition",     x = NULL, y = "Energy (kcal/mol)", fill = NULL   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     legend.position = "bottom"   )

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: Glide 同时输出 CSV 和 .maegz，用 CSV 验证解析器的准确性：
code:
csv_file <- "/path/to/your/BRD4_Indomethacin_SP.csv" csv_data <- read.csv(csv_file)  csv_scores <- sort(round(csv_data$r_i_glide_gscore, 3)) mae_scores <- sort(round(poses$glide_gscore, 3))  cat("CSV: ", csv_scores, "
") cat("MAE: ", mae_scores, "
") cat("Match:", identical(csv_scores, mae_scores), "
")

title: rschrodinger: Schrödinger Data Bridge for R
text: .mae 是文本格式。R 的字符串处理完全够用，C++ 增加编译依赖，在 HPC 上部署困难。 Schrödinger Python 绑定了自己的 NumPy/OpenMM，和 conda 冲突。processx 起干净子进程，JSON 传数据，零 ABI 风险。

what: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: rschrodinger attempt recreate entire Schrödinger suite Python API R. Instead, small, fast, focused data bridge designed specifically AIDD (AI-driven Drug Discovery) developers need seamless data transit Schrödinger’s computational engines R’s robust statistical bioinformatics ecosystems.

what: Why this package exists (The 30% Time Sink)
title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: modern CADD pipelines, Schrödinger dominates computation (Docking, MD, QM), downstream biological statistics, visualization, modeling often happen R Python. Currently, R users stranded: must rely clunky shell scripts, intermediate .sdf/.csv file conversions via structconvert, wrestle environment-destroying Python reticulate configurations. package provides pragmatic, architecture-level solution bypass three massive hurdles wrapping Schrödinger: 1. Environment Nightmare: Calling $SCHRODINGER/run aggressively overrides system dynamic libraries (libstdc++, etc.), causing silent segfaults linked alongside Rcpp data.table. 2. License Blackbox (SLM): Deeply wrapping C++ instances triggers Schrödinger License Manager (SLM) checks. Navigating via unapproved bridges technically fragile legally risky (EULA violations). 3. .mae Format Abyss: Maestro files (.mae) highly nested, regex-based R parsers inevitably drop critical data like Epik protonation states Glide score components.

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: “Don’t rebuild Schrödinger console R. Build pipeline.” strictly avoided “thick wrappers” attempt inherit C++ memory ownership rewrite algorithm dispatchers. rschrodinger focuses three core pillars:

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: Bypasses structconvert -> SDF -> R bottleneck entirely. Built Rcpp + maeparser (Schrödinger’s MIT-licensed C++ parser), safely extracts molecular structures, interaction topologies, custom properties (like r_i_glide_gscore) directly R tibble/data.table. - Zero License Risk (pure open-source C++) - Zero Data Loss (direct parsing, intermediate formats)

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: heavily isolated micro-bridge using reticulate invoke Schrödinger’s schrodinger.structutils.interactions.pi. executes completely inside ephemeral, strictly sandboxed $SCHRODINGER/run background process, returning neat data.frame distances, angles, Pi-stack contacts, immediately destroying context protect R host environment.

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: Ditch flaky file-timestamp checking. clean REST-like R wrapper schrodinger.job.jobcontrol check job IDs return standardized statuses (ACTIVE, COMPLETED, DIED) resilient workflow automation.

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
text: can install development version rschrodinger GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("jixing475/rschrodinger")

title: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R
code: library(rschrodinger)  # 1. Ultra-fast native .mae reading into a tibble hits_df <- read_mae_fast("docking_results.maegz", properties = c("r_i_glide_gscore", "s_m_title"))  # 2. Extract detailed protein-ligand interactions safely interactions <- get_protein_ligand_interactions("complex.maegz")  # 3. Check HPC job status status <- poll_job_status("localhost-0-61a8b9")

title: Extract Protein-Ligand Interactions — get_protein_ligand_interactions
text: Extract protein-ligand interactions complex structure using Schrodinger's Python API sandboxed subprocess. Requires Schrodinger installation.

title: Extract Protein-Ligand Interactions — get_protein_ligand_interactions
code: get_protein_ligand_interactions(   complex_file,   schrodinger_path = Sys.getenv("SCHRODINGER") )

title: Extract Protein-Ligand Interactions — get_protein_ligand_interactions
text: complex_file Character. Path complex structure file (.mae .pdb). schrodinger_path Character. Path Schrodinger installation directory. Defaults SCHRODINGER environment variable.

title: Extract Protein-Ligand Interactions — get_protein_ligand_interactions
text: data.frame columns: interaction_type, residue, distance, additional details depending interaction type.

title: Extract Protein-Ligand Interactions — get_protein_ligand_interactions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ interactions <- get_protein_ligand_interactions("complex.mae") } # }

title: Poll Schrodinger Job Status — poll_job_status
text: Query Schrodinger job status HPC workflow integration. Optionally block job completes times .

title: Poll Schrodinger Job Status — poll_job_status
code: poll_job_status(   job_id,   schrodinger_path = Sys.getenv("SCHRODINGER"),   wait = FALSE,   timeout = 3600,   interval = 30 )

title: Poll Schrodinger Job Status — poll_job_status
text: job_id Character. Schrodinger job ID. schrodinger_path Character. Path Schrodinger installation. Defaults SCHRODINGER environment variable. wait Logical. TRUE, block job completes timeout. Default FALSE. timeout Numeric. Max seconds wait. Default 3600 (1 hour). interval Numeric. Seconds polls waiting. Default 30.

title: Poll Schrodinger Job Status — poll_job_status
text: list components: status Character: "running", "completed", "failed", "unknown". job_id queried job ID. details Raw details jobcontrol (list).

title: Poll Schrodinger Job Status — poll_job_status
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ poll_job_status("my_job_12345") poll_job_status("my_job_12345", wait = TRUE, timeout = 600) } # }

title: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) Files — read_mae_fast
text: Parse Schrodinger Maestro format files natively R without requiring Schrodinger installation. Extracts CT (Connection Table) level properties tidy tibble.

title: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) Files — read_mae_fast
code: read_mae_fast(   path,   properties = NULL,   strip_prefix = TRUE,   include_atoms = FALSE )

title: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) Files — read_mae_fast
text: path Character. Path .mae .maegz file, directory (reads .mae/.maegz files ). properties Character vector NULL. specified, extract properties (matched prefix stripping strip_prefix TRUE). NULL extracts CT-level properties. strip_prefix Logical. TRUE (default), strip m2io type namespace prefixes property names (e.g., r_i_glide_gscore becomes glide_gscore). include_atoms Logical. TRUE, include atom data list-column named atoms. Default FALSE.

title: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) Files — read_mae_fast
text: tibble row one CT (Connection Table) entry. Columns extracted properties, plus _ct_index (integer) _source_file (character) tracking provenance.

title: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) Files — read_mae_fast
code: mae_file <- system.file("extdata", "example.mae",   package = "rschrodinger", mustWork = TRUE) read_mae_fast(mae_file) #> # A tibble: 1 × 19 #>   title PDB_CRYST1_a PDB_CRYST1_b PDB_CRYST1_c PDB_CRYST1_alpha PDB_CRYST1_beta #>   <chr>        <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>            <dbl>           <dbl> #> 1 3MXF          36.8         44.8         78.4               90              90 #> # ℹ 13 more variables: PDB_CRYST1_gamma <dbl>, PDB_CRYST1_z <int>, #> #   source_file_index <int>, PDB_TITLE <chr>, PDB_ID <chr>, #> #   PDB_CRYST1_Space_Group <chr>, PDB_CLASSIFICATION <chr>, #> #   PDB_DEPOSITION_DATE <chr>, PDB_format_version <chr>, source_file <chr>, #> #   ct_format <int>, `_ct_index` <int>, `_source_file` <chr>

what: rschrodinger: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R — rschrodinger-package
title: rschrodinger: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R — rschrodinger-package
text: Read Maestro (.mae/.maegz) files natively R without requiring Schrodinger installation. Optionally bridge Schrodinger's Python API protein-ligand interaction analysis job monitoring via sandboxed subprocess calls.

title: rschrodinger: Fast Data Bridge Between Schrodinger and R — rschrodinger-package
text: Maintainer: Jixing Liu jixing.liu@example.com [copyright holder]

title: rschrodinger 0.1.0
text: Initial release. State-machine parser, zero Schrödinger dependency. Automatic type conversion based property name prefixes. Prefix stripping clean column names. Directory scanning batch processing. Atom data extraction via include_atoms parameter. get_protein_ligand_interactions(): Extract protein-ligand interactions via sandboxed Schrödinger Python subprocess. poll_job_status(): Monitor Schrödinger HPC job status optional blocking wait configurable timeout.
